threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still bewildered by the bus routes and ask for directions to the nearest tube, while everyone else breezes past on their bikes.
Last night on the 68, a guy in a parrot costume serenaded everyone with sea shanties. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a street musician playing an accordion in front of the Späti, and a group of kids joined in to dance. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Kebab stand that serves vegan döners with a side of techno beats at 2 AM. It's like a late-night pilgrimage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can spot the difference between a regular fog and the 'Karl the Fog' hype. Tourists always take a photo.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 14 who turned his phone into a karaoke machine, belting out classic rock. The other riders just rolled their eyes, but I loved it.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are on Carrer de Verdi. But the hidden gems are in the back alleys — places where you’ll be the only foreigner.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’ll be the ones at the local market struggling to order in broken Catalan, while clutching a tourist guide like it's a life raft.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver calls out street names like they're tongue twisters – we all learn to just nod along.
Caught the 196 last night; a guy in a bright yellow jacket kept asking everyone if they preferred pineapple on their pizza. Oddly specific mix of cheeky and charming.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a spontaneous dance party break out at the Karl-Marx-Platz, with strangers joining in like it was an everyday occurrence.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can spot a spontaneous art installation on the U-Bahn platforms here. One day it’s a wall of flowers, the next it’s a graffiti mural evolving in real-time.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name two taco spots without skipping a beat, but get flustered by BART delays. That's just a classic SF day.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a saxophone on the 31, serenading a random couple. They just smiled and kept scrolling on their phones. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the plazas, but the real charm is the random street art hidden in alleyways at night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always look up at the street signs, trying to decipher the mix of Catalan and Spanish. A true local knows to trust their gut over Google Maps.
